question: Are all hikers now concentrated in the lower/front portion of the boardwalk?
Is the back half of the boardwalk trail (upper section) completely empty of hikers, with no partial bodies or remnants remaining?
Have the hikers moved from the background been resized larger (including their gear) to match the perspective of the foreground?
Are the relocated hikers arranged naturally without physically impossible overlaps or collisions?
Do the occlusion relationships between the gathered hikers appear logically consistent (closer figures properly blocking view of those behind)?
Do the gathered hikers fit within the width of the boardwalk without stepping off into the vegetation or floating over the edge?
Are the relocated hikers oriented naturally, facing directions consistent with walking along the boardwalk?
Do the relocated hikers appear firmly grounded on the planks rather than floating above or sinking into the wood?
Is the wooden plank texture in the cleared upper section of the boardwalk restored seamlessly without blur or pattern repetition?
Are the railings in the areas where hikers were removed reconstructed continuously without breaks or distortions?
Do the relocated hikers in the foreground cast realistic shadows consistent with the scene's lighting direction?
Is the lighting on the relocated hikers harmonized to match the brightness and color temperature of the foreground environment?
Are the relocated hikers free from anatomical anomalies like floating limbs, missing feet, or duplicated body parts?
Is the image free from visible artifacts such as ghosting, halos, or hard cutout edges around the moved figures?
